title: Executive Order 10842—Creating a Board of Inquiry To Report on Certain Labor Disputes Affecting the Maritime Industry of the United States

flags.justification: A three-member Board whose powers and duties are defined entirely by statute (Title II of the Labor-Management Relations Act), not created ad hoc by this order.
A clear, statutorily-required reporting deadline (October 10, 1959) to the President.
Single purpose: inquiry into one set of related maritime labor disputes.
The Board's powers and duties are precisely bounded by Title II of the LMRA.
No incentive structure rewarding behavior contrary to the order's purpose is identifiable.

Unlike several comparably-scoped advisory bodies in this corpus (EO 9387, EO 9808, EO 11076), the Board does not automatically dissolve upon submitting its report — it 'shall continue in existence... until the Board is terminated by the President,' requiring affirmative future action rather than self-executing termination.
This exercises the Taft-Hartley Title II process exactly as Congress designed it, including its judicial injunction procedures — an added statutory check, not a removal of one.
The LMRA's Title II process includes judicial injunction procedures providing a genuine external check.
A routine application of an already well-established statutory mechanism used many times by 1959; not a novel authority claim.
No second agency or mandate is implicated.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.
